cards.name: Join Zsolt Jozsef Simon — Porcelain Casting Workshop at Art & Design Building
cards.blurb: Grab something sweet at Swana Sweets while you're out on the Student Union patio and soak in the mild morning air.
cards.why: Perfect way to kick off a campus-centered day.

cards.name: Introduction to Python and Pandas at King Library
cards.blurb: Drop into this free Python and Pandas intro at King Library. No laptop? Just show up and see what it's about.
cards.why: Free, practical, and you'll actually walk away knowing something new.

cards.name: R&R, 2026: Recovering Ricardo Favela’s Royal Chicano Air Force Poster Collection at San José State University at King Library
cards.blurb: Browse the R&R, 2026 exhibition at King Library, a showcase of recovered posters from Ricardo Favela's Royal Chicano Air Force collection. Local history with serious visual punch.
cards.why: It's free art with real cultural weight right there on campus.

cards.name: Be Ready: Be Prepared for Disasters
cards.blurb: Swing by this disaster preparedness talk and actually get some useful info on what to do when things go sideways in the South Bay.
cards.why: Free and genuinely useful knowledge you'll be glad you have.

cards.name: Natalie and James Thompson Gallery: Click on My Mouth: Language and Technology at ART 127: Thompson Gallery
cards.blurb: Check out Click on My Mouth: Language and Technology at the Thompson Gallery. It's a thought-provoking show exploring how tech shapes the way we communicate.
cards.why: It's the kind of free art show that actually gives you something to talk about over dinner.

cards.name: San Jose Giants vs. Visalia Rawhide
cards.blurb: Head to the ballpark for San Jose Giants vs. Visalia Rawhide. Get there early for decent seats, grab a beer and some ballpark food, and settle in for nine innings under the lights.
cards.why: A Thursday night baseball game on a 72-degree day is hard to beat.

cards.name: Anthony Jeselnik
cards.blurb: If the game wraps up early or you're in the mood to keep the night going, Anthony Jeselnik is performing at 62 S Second St tonight. Dark humor, sharp delivery, zero chill.
cards.why: He's one of the best working comics right now and he's right here tonight.

cards.name: Spark60: Meditations for Mortals: The Liberating Art of Embracing Your Limits with Oliver Burkeman
cards.blurb: Catch Oliver Burkeman talk about embracing limits and doing less — it's a surprisingly refreshing way to kick off a Thursday morning.
cards.why: Free, thoughtful, and a genuinely good excuse to be out early.

cards.name: Market Street Spring Jubilee
cards.blurb: Hit the Market Street Spring Jubilee for live music and the general vibe of a street fest on a warm afternoon.
cards.why: 70 degrees and live music outside is pretty much the ideal Thursday.

cards.name: Jessica Monette: Root Me in the Soil, In the Wake and the Becoming (Project Room Exhibition)
cards.blurb: Wander through Jessica Monette's project room exhibition at SCU — soil, roots, and becoming, all in one quiet gallery space.
cards.why: It's free art you can actually stand in front of and think about.

cards.name: AI-Powered tools to help with your searching
cards.blurb: Drop into this free session on AI-powered search tools — practical, fast, and you'll probably leave knowing something useful.
cards.why: It's the kind of low-key skill upgrade that actually sticks.

cards.name: Anthony Jeselnik
cards.blurb: Anthony Jeselnik live at 62 S Second St in San Jose. Dark, sharp comedy that does not pull punches.
cards.why: He's genuinely one of the best working stand-ups right now.

cards.name: Ethics Certificate Training for Students: April 9 & 16
cards.blurb: Sit in on the Ethics Certificate Training at SCU — even if you're not a student, the conversations in these sessions tend to be worth it.
cards.why: Free, thought-provoking, and a good way to fill the early evening.

cards.name: Chicago Presidential Reception
cards.blurb: Swing by the Chicago Presidential Reception at SCU — free event, good energy, and a solid reason to put on real clothes.
cards.why: You don't need a reason beyond free food and interesting people.

cards.name: Vibe & Thrive Mini Health Fair at Science Lawn
cards.blurb: Check out the Vibe & Thrive Mini Health Fair on the Science Lawn — early bird hours mean shorter lines and a chill vibe to ease into the morning.
cards.why: It's happening right now and it's free, so why not kick off the day with something different.

cards.name: Spark60: Meditations for Mortals: The Liberating Art of Embracing Your Limits with Oliver Burkeman
cards.blurb: Catch Oliver Burkeman's Spark60 talk on embracing your limits. It's a lecture-style format, so grab a seat and actually sit with the ideas he's throwing out.
cards.why: A talk about doing less and living better hits different first thing in the morning.

cards.name: Market Street Spring Jubilee
cards.blurb: Head to the Market Street Spring Jubilee for live music and the general good energy of a street festival on a 72-degree afternoon.
cards.why: Live music outside in perfect weather is hard to beat on a Thursday.

cards.name: Jessica Monette: Root Me in the Soil, In the Wake and the Becoming (Project Room Exhibition)
cards.blurb: Walk through Jessica Monette's Project Room Exhibition, Root Me in the Soil, In the Wake and the Becoming. Take your time with it; the work rewards slow looking.
cards.why: Free art that's actually worth the stop, not just filler.

cards.name: Be Ready: Be Prepared for Disasters
cards.blurb: Pop into the Be Ready: Be Prepared for Disasters community session. It's practical, genuinely useful info, and worth having if you live in the Bay Area.
cards.why: You've been meaning to actually learn this stuff, and now there's no excuse.

cards.name: Anthony Jeselnik
cards.blurb: Anthony Jeselnik live at 62 S Second St. He's sharp, dark, and doesn't hold back, so go in knowing exactly what you're signing up for.
cards.why: Stand-up comedy on a Thursday night is the right call.

cards.name: GIS & Drone Society Club Meeting
cards.blurb: Swing by the GIS & Drone Society Club Meeting if you're at all curious about mapping tech or drones. It's low-key and open.
cards.why: Good way to fill the early evening with something genuinely nerdy and fun.

cards.name: Homework Help for Grades K-6
cards.blurb: Join the Qigong class at Cupertino Library — a low-key, grounding way to ease into the day with some gentle movement.
cards.why: Starting the morning with intentional movement sets a great tone for the rest of the day.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Main Street Cupertino and grab lunch at Lazy Dog or one of the Asian spots along the plaza — walk the outdoor space after and enjoy the afternoon air.
cards.why: It's right in Cupertino and the patio vibes are perfect for a mild April afternoon.

cards.name: AI-Powered tools to help with your searching
cards.blurb: Catch this free talk at Santa Clara University on AI-powered search tools — practical, interesting, and worth knowing about even if you're not a tech person.
cards.why: Free, genuinely useful info and a good way to spend an hour and a half after lunch.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Wander through the Triton Museum of Art — check out the contemporary galleries and spend some time in the sculpture garden before they close.
cards.why: Free world-class art that most people in the South Bay have never been to. Worth it every time.

cards.name: Bakenstein
cards.address: 1370 Homestead Rd, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into Bakenstein for something sweet before the evening winds up — rated 4.9 and worth every bite.
cards.why: A pre-meditation treat from one of the highest-rated spots in Santa Clara is a solid call.

cards.name: Thursday Evening Meditation and Mindfulness
cards.blurb: End the day at Thursday Evening Meditation and Mindfulness in Cupertino. Drop in, decompress, and let the week melt off.
cards.why: Bookending a Qigong morning with an evening meditation session is a genuinely great Thursday.

cards.name: Spotlight Tours Thursdays | Anderson Collection
cards.blurb: Join the Spotlight Tour at the Anderson Collection and let a docent walk you through the highlights of one of the best collections of postwar American art in the country.
cards.why: Free, no tickets needed, and a great warm-up before you come back to draw later.

cards.name: 1,000 Ways to Hold: A New Work By Erika Chong Shuch
cards.blurb: Catch this new performance piece by Erika Chong Shuch at the Bing Studio — it's the kind of intimate, experimental work that's worth seeing without knowing too much going in.
cards.why: Free and it's happening right now on campus, so just go.

cards.name: Graphic Novel Book Club
cards.blurb: Head to the Graphic Novel Book Club. Even if you didn't read the book, these things are usually pretty welcoming and the conversation gets good fast.
cards.why: A free, low-key social hour that breaks up the visual art heavy evening nicely.

cards.name: Friends of The Library Membership Month Book Sale - Friends Preview Sale (w/ Annual Meeting)
cards.blurb: Browse the Friends Preview Sale before it opens to the general public — this is the good shift where you actually find stuff worth buying.
cards.why: Free books, good crowd, and you get first pick.

cards.name: Bloomsgiving
cards.address: 301 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Grab dinner here on Castro St — it's the kind of spot that feels right for a Thursday evening when you want something good without a whole production.
cards.why: Solid food, chill vibe, and they close at 8 so you're getting in at the right time.

cards.name: Pelicans, Herons, and Egrets, Oh My!
cards.blurb: Head to this free talk about pelicans, herons, and egrets in Los Altos. Whether you're a birder or just Bay-Area-curious, it's a genuinely interesting way to spend an evening.
cards.why: Free, educational, and way more fun than it sounds.

cards.name: Toddler Time
cards.blurb: Catch Read Me, the arts event in Mountain View. Show up, take it in, and see what's on the walls or stage tonight.
cards.why: It's a locked item for a reason — local arts nights like this are worth the effort.

cards.name: Books Inc.
cards.address: 317 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down with a quick stop at Books Inc. on Castro. Browse whatever's on the staff picks table and grab something to read this weekend.
cards.why: Open until 10 and it's the perfect low-key cap to the night.

cards.name: Drop-In Drawing @ The Anderson
cards.blurb: Drop in and draw at the Anderson Collection — no prep needed, just show up and sketch in one of Stanford's best art spaces.
cards.why: Free, low-key, and a genuinely nice way to spend a Thursday morning.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.blurb: Walk the Rodin sculpture garden, then head inside to browse the permanent collection and whatever's currently on view. It's free and genuinely world-class.
cards.why: You're already on campus and this is one of the best free museums in the Bay Area.

cards.name: Lou Henry Hoover Carillon Performance
cards.blurb: Catch the Lou Henry Hoover Carillon performance on campus. It's a short stop but the bells filling the Stanford air is a pretty surreal midday moment.
cards.why: It's happening today and it costs almost nothing to swing by.

cards.name: Spotlight Tours Thursdays | Anderson Collection
cards.blurb: Join the Thursday Spotlight Tour at the Anderson Collection and get a guided look at the pieces you just walked past on your own.
cards.why: Having a docent break down the collection makes it stick in a way solo browsing doesn't.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.blurb: Head up the Dish Trail for the late afternoon loop. The light at this hour is great and you'll get those wide foothill views looking out over the Bay.
cards.why: 72°F and sunny is basically the ideal Dish weather.

cards.name: Graphic Novel Book Club
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening at the Graphic Novel Book Club. Even if you haven't read this month's pick, it's the kind of crowd where that's fine.
cards.why: Good conversation with people who take comics seriously is a solid Thursday night.
